title: Gemini extension manifest (gemini-extension.json)
description: Synthesized from the upstream ExtensionConfig TypeScript interface at packages/cli/src/config/extension.ts and the related ExtensionSetting / CustomTheme types in packages/core/src/config/config.ts (google-gemini/gemini-cli repo). Component subdirectories (commands/, agents/, hooks/, skills/, policies/) are conventions, not declared in this manifest.

name.description: Extension identifier — lowercase, numbers, and dashes only. Must match the extension's directory name. This is what users type to refer to the extension in the CLI.

mcpServers.description: Map of name → MCP server config. Same shape as settings.json mcpServers, with one exception: `trust` is NOT supported in extension-defined MCP servers. Use ${extensionPath} for paths, and split executable + args via `command` + `args`.

trust.description: NOT supported in extension-defined MCP servers (per Gemini docs). Use settings.json mcpServers if you need trust. The schema rejects this property here.

contextFileName.description: File(s) loaded as context. Accepts a string or string array. Defaults to 'GEMINI.md' if a file by that name exists in the extension directory and this property is omitted.

excludeTools.description: Tools the extension forbids. Supports command-specific restrictions for tools that allow it, e.g. `run_shell_command(rm -rf)` blocks just the `rm -rf` command rather than the entire tool. Distinct from per-MCP-server `excludeTools` listed inside the MCP server config.

envVar.description: Environment variable name backing the setting.

sensitive.description: Whether the value is sensitive (e.g. a token).

themes.description: Custom themes contributed by this extension. Registered when the extension is activated.

plan.description: Planning features configuration contributed by this extension.

directory.description: Where the extension stores plan artifacts.

migratedTo.description: New repository URL when the extension has been migrated.
